symbols.name: n
symbols.def: Sample size.

symbols.name: d
symbols.def: Dimension of the covariate vector X.

symbols.name: O
symbols.space: {(Y,A,X): Y in R, A in [0,1], X in [0,1]^d}
symbols.def: Observed data unit O = (Y, A, X).

symbols.name: O_1, ..., O_n
symbols.def: Observed iid sample of size n.

symbols.name: Y
symbols.def: Observed outcome.

symbols.name: A
symbols.def: Observed continuous treatment.

symbols.name: X
symbols.def: Observed covariate vector.

symbols.name: P
symbols.def: Observed-data law of O.

symbols.name: P_X
symbols.def: Marginal law of X induced by P.

symbols.name: Y(a)
symbols.def: Potential outcome under treatment level a.

symbols.name: t_0
symbols.def: Interior treatment level at which the dose-response is evaluated.

symbols.name: epsilon_0
symbols.def: Radius of the interior neighborhood around t_0 on which positivity and treatment-direction smoothness are imposed.

symbols.name: alpha
symbols.def: Holder smoothness order of a -> mu_P(a,x) on [t_0 - epsilon_0, t_0 + epsilon_0].

symbols.name: beta
symbols.def: Holder smoothness order of a -> pi_P(a|x) on [t_0 - epsilon_0, t_0 + epsilon_0].

symbols.name: s
symbols.def: Common Holder smoothness order in the covariate direction for x -> mu_P(t_0,x), x -> pi_P(t_0|x), and the density of P_X.

symbols.name: M
symbols.def: Generic envelope constant for boundedness and Holder radii.

symbols.name: c_0
symbols.def: Lower positivity bound in the neighborhood of t_0.

symbols.name: mu_P
symbols.def: mu_P(a,x) = E_P[Y | A = a, X = x].

symbols.name: pi_P
symbols.def: pi_P(a|x) is the conditional density of A given X = x under P.

symbols.name: p_{X,P}
symbols.def: Density of P_X with respect to Lebesgue measure.

symbols.name: theta_P(t_0)
symbols.def: theta_P(t_0) = E_P[mu_P(t_0,X)] = E_P[Y(t_0)] under the causal assumptions.

symbols.name: R_n
symbols.def: Pointwise minimax mean-squared risk for estimating theta_P(t_0).

symbols.name: rho_n
symbols.def: Candidate sharp pointwise minimax MSE sequence in the beta >= alpha regime.

assumptions.condition: O_1, ..., O_n are iid draws from P.
Y = Y(A) almost surely under P.
For every a in [0,1], Y(a) is independent of A conditional on X under P.
[t_0 - epsilon_0, t_0 + epsilon_0] is contained in (0,1).
pi_P(a|x) >= c_0 for every a in [t_0 - epsilon_0, t_0 + epsilon_0] and every x in [0,1]^d.
For every x in [0,1]^d, the function a -> mu_P(a,x) belongs to a Holder ball of order alpha and radius M on [t_0 - epsilon_0, t_0 + epsilon_0].

standard.name: Holder smoothness in the treatment coordinate

assumptions.condition: For every x in [0,1]^d, the function a -> pi_P(a|x) belongs to a Holder ball of order beta and radius M on [t_0 - epsilon_0, t_0 + epsilon_0].

standard.name: Holder smoothness of the conditional treatment density in the treatment coordinate

assumptions.condition: The function x -> mu_P(t_0,x) belongs to a Holder ball of order s and radius M on [0,1]^d.

standard.name: Holder smoothness in the covariate coordinate

assumptions.condition: The function x -> pi_P(t_0|x) belongs to a Holder ball of order s and radius M on [0,1]^d.

standard.name: Holder smoothness of the conditional treatment density in the covariate coordinate

assumptions.condition: The density p_{X,P} belongs to a Holder ball of order s and radius M on [0,1]^d and is bounded above by M.

novel.justification: This is the clean Bonvini-Kennedy subregime where the published HOIF upper rate is already interpretable in terms of the dose-response smoothness alpha. It is realistic in continuous-dose settings where the assignment density is smoother in dose than the response surface, such as Gaussian or log-concave treatment rules with smooth conditional means.

assumptions.condition: There exist a density p_0 on [0,1]^d, a conditional treatment density q_0 on [0,1], and a constant eta_0>0 such that p_0 and q_0 have Holder norms at most M-eta_0 in the smoothness classes required of p_{X,P} and pi_P, q_0(a) >= c_0+eta_0 for all a in [t_0-epsilon_0,t_0+epsilon_0], and p_0 is bounded above by M-eta_0.

novel.justification: standard: nonempty interior-of-model condition used in minimax lower bounds for Holder balls and overlap classes; cf. Tsybakov (2009, Nonparametric Estimation, lower-bound constructions) and Kennedy, Balakrishnan, Robins, Wasserman (2022, minimax causal lower bounds).

definitions.name: P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0)
definitions.construction: {P : P satisfies ass:iid-sampling, ass:consistency, ass:no-unmeasured-confounding, ass:bounded-outcome, ass:interior-dose, ass:local-positivity, ass:mu-treatment-holder, ass:pi-treatment-holder, ass:mu-covariate-holder, ass:pi-covariate-holder, ass:px-holder}

definitions.name: R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0), t_0)
definitions.construction: R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0), t_0) = inf_{hat_theta_n} sup_{P in P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0)} E_P[(hat_theta_n - theta_P(t_0))^2]

definitions.name: beta-frontier handle
definitions.construction: Headline and frontier handle. The delivered results are: (1) the first all-beta minimax lower bound R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0),t_0) >= c n^{-2*alpha/(2*alpha+1)} over the interior Holder dose-response class, by a de-laundered two-point Le Cam construction perturbing only mu_P with the covariate density and treatment-density baselines fixed; (2) the sharp same-class minimax characterization R_n asymp n^{-2*alpha/(2*alpha+1)} in the smooth-covariate regime s>=d/4, matched by an explicit self-contained cross-fitted projection-HOIF estimator on the same class; and (3) for 0<s<d/4 and fixed beta>0, the honest same-class bracket c n^{-2*alpha/(2*alpha+1)} <= R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0),t_0) <= C n^{-2/(1+d/(4*s)+1/alpha)} obtained by re-optimizing the projection-HOIF sieve dimension. The terminal residual carried by oeq:full-beta-frontier is only the sharp deficient-regime exponent inside this bracket; no matching converse for the functional branch is asserted.

statements.statement: Assume ass:baseline-submodel-slack. Then for every beta>0 there exists a constant c > 0, depending only on the fixed model radii and the slack baseline, such that for all sufficiently large n, R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0), t_0) >= c n^(-2*alpha/(2*alpha+1)). Equivalently, uniformly over every beta>0, every estimator of theta_P(t_0) over P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0) has worst-case mean-squared error at least a constant multiple of the classical interior treatment-regression pointwise rate.
statements.justification: This is the missing converse to the Bonvini-Kennedy HOIF upper bound in the clean beta >= alpha regime where the published rate is already explicit.
statements.gap: BonviniKennedy2022FastDRF gives the fastest published upper rate but explicitly states that a tight lower bound remains elusive; KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2022MinimaxCATE supplies the closest fuzzy-hypothesis template, but not the continuous-dose localization with density normalization.
statements.consumer: It closes the optimality gap for interior fixed-dose estimation and turns the Bonvini-Kennedy HOIF rate into a certified benchmark for later estimator design, tuning, and adaptation work.

statements.statement: Under the assumptions of thm:sharp-pointwise-lower-bound, if s >= d/4 then rho_n = n^(-2*alpha/(2*alpha+1)) and therefore R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0), t_0) >= c n^(-2*alpha/(2*alpha+1)) for some c > 0. Thus the lower frontier reduces to the classical interior pointwise nonparametric regression barrier.
statements.justification: A sharp converse needs a transparent sanity reduction showing that once the covariate nuisances are smooth enough, no causal complication beats the ordinary local-regression difficulty.
statements.gap: The reduction is implicit in BonviniKennedy2022FastDRF and classical regression theory, but the interior dose-response literature does not currently state it as part of a unified minimax frontier.
statements.consumer: It gives the referee-visible special case that collapses to a known result and clarifies exactly when the nuisance-product obstruction disappears.

statements.statement: Certified partial resolution of the unrestricted beta frontier. Under ass:baseline-submodel-slack, if s>=d/4 then for every beta>0 the original class P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0) has sharp pointwise minimax MSE R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0),t_0) asymp n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)}=rho_n. If 0<s<d/4, then for every fixed beta>0 the original class satisfies the proved bracket c n^{-2 alpha/(2 alpha+1)} <= R_n(P_{alpha,beta,s}(M,c_0,epsilon_0,t_0),t_0) <= C n^{-2/(1+d/(4s)+1/alpha)}=C rho_n for all large n. The remaining open question is to determine the sharp deficient-regime exponent within this bracket: the oracle floor, the HOIF functional branch, an intermediate exponent, or a genuinely beta-sensitive phase.
statements.justification: Bonvini-Kennedy themselves note that the treatment-density smoothness beta may enter their upper bound only through proof bookkeeping, so the full beta-frontier is the real unresolved mathematical content beyond the clean subregime.
statements.gap: No published paper determines whether the beta dependence in continuous-dose HOIF rates is structural or an artifact of the current upper-bound argument.
statements.consumer: Resolving this question would complete the anisotropic phase diagram and determine whether future adaptive confidence procedures must learn beta as a genuine hardness parameter.

sampling_model.treatment: A_i is a scalar continuous treatment supported on [0,1]
sampling_model.overlap: The conditional treatment density pi_P(a|x) is bounded below on an epsilon_0-neighborhood of the interior dose t_0
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: No estimator is built into the class; nuisance difficulty is encoded by Holder regularity of mu_P, pi_P, and p_{X,P}

Kennedy, E. H., Balakrishnan, S., Robins, J. M., and Wasserman, L. (2022). Minimax rates for heterogeneous causal effect estimation. arXiv:2203.00837.
Robins, J. M., Li, L., Tchetgen Tchetgen, E., and van der Vaart, A. (2008). Higher order influence functions and minimax estimation of nonlinear functionals. arXiv:0805.3040.
Robins, J. M., Li, L., Tchetgen Tchetgen, E., and van der Vaart, A. (2016). Technical report: Higher order influence functions and minimax estimation of nonlinear functionals. arXiv:1601.05820.
Colangelo, K., and Lee, Y.-Y. (2020). Double debiased machine learning nonparametric inference with continuous treatments. arXiv:2004.03036.
Semenova, V., and Chernozhukov, V. (2017). Debiased machine learning of conditional average treatment effects and other causal functions. arXiv:1702.06240.

Kennedy, E. H., Ma, Z., McHugh, M. D., and Small, D. S. (2017). Nonparametric methods for doubly robust estimation of continuous treatment effects. Journal of the Royal Statistical Society, Series B 79(4), 1229-1245.
Schindl, K., Shen, S., and Kennedy, E. H. (2024). Incremental effects for continuous exposures. arXiv:2409.11967.

Cinelli, C., Feller, A., Imbens, G., Kennedy, E., Magliacane, S., and Zubizarreta, J. (2025). Challenges in statistics: A dozen challenges in causality and causal inference. arXiv:2508.17099.
Tsybakov, A. B. (2009). Introduction to Nonparametric Estimation. Springer.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: The higher-order dose-response estimator attains the rate n^(-2*alpha/(2*alpha+1)) vee n^(-2/(1 + d/(4*s) + 1/alpha)) in the beta >= alpha regime when the higher-order remainder is negligible.
Minimax CATE lower bounds are proved by localized fuzzy hypotheses that mix regression and nonlinear-functional difficulty.
Kernel-localized DML delivers asymptotic normality for interior continuous-treatment effects under high-level nuisance conditions.
Pseudo-outcome local regression yields doubly robust continuous-treatment dose-response estimation with second-order remainder control.
Orthogonal-signal projection delivers estimation and inference for causal functionals through best linear prediction.
Higher-order influence functions yield rate-optimal estimation of nonlinear functionals in nonparametric models.
Higher-order influence-function calculus and product expansions justify the HOIF bias-variance frontier used by later causal estimators.
Incremental continuous-exposure lower bounds scale with an effective sample size n/delta and yield edge-dose rates as delta tends to infinity.
Data-adaptive smoothing can deliver almost-optimal confidence intervals for nonregular parameters such as fixed-dose dose-response values.

tldr: The proposal targets the missing converse for interior continuous-treatment dose-response estimation at a fixed dose t_0. Bonvini and Kennedy derive the fastest published HOIF upper rate and explicitly leave the minimax lower bound open. The kernel here is to prove that, on a clean anisotropic Holder class with common covariate smoothness s and treatment-density smoothness at least as large as the dose-response smoothness alpha, every estimator of theta_P(t_0) has worst-case MSE at least n^(-2*alpha/(2*alpha+1)) vee n^(-2/(1 + d/(4*s) + 1/alpha)). That theorem would certify the published HOIF rate as sharp in the beta >= alpha regime, while the honest open content is whether a distinct beta-dominated phase appears once beta < alpha is allowed.

project_justification.gap: The interior fixed-dose dose-response literature has a published higher-order upper rate but no matching minimax converse, so the central frontier is still one-sided.
project_justification.niche: The target theta_P(t_0) is neither ordinary regression nor an ordinary nonlinear functional: the hard subexperiment must localize in the treatment coordinate while preserving conditional-density normalization and positivity.
project_justification.fill: The project fills that gap by adapting the CATE fuzzy-hypothesis architecture to a continuous-dose partial-mean target, proving the matching pointwise lower bound in the beta >= alpha regime, and isolating the unresolved beta-frontier as a separate open question rather than hiding it in a vague future-work sentence.

related_work: BonviniKennedy2022FastDRF is the anchor because it introduces the continuous-dose HOIF estimator, separates treatment-direction and covariate-direction smoothness, and states that a tight lower bound remains elusive. KennedyMaMcHughSmall2017ContinuousDR is the first pseudo-outcome dose-response precursor because it establishes the regression-plus-nuisance-remainder representation that later estimators refine. ColangeloLee2020ContinuousDML and SemenovaChernozhukov2017CausalFunctions are the main first-order comparators: both provide orthogonal-score or orthogonal-signal inference machinery for continuous or function-valued causal targets, but neither characterizes the minimax frontier for interior fixed-dose estimation. KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2022MinimaxCATE is the closest lower-bound template because it proves a hybrid regression-functional rate by localized fuzzy hypotheses, yet its construction does not enforce continuous-treatment density normalization or fixed-dose localization. RobinsLiTchetgenVanderVaart2008HOIF and RobinsLiTchetgenVanderVaart2016HOIFTechnical provide the higher-order von Mises and U-statistic logic underlying the Bonvini-Kennedy upper bound that the converse must match. SchindlShenKennedy2024IncrementalContinuous is the natural foil because it delivers genuine continuous-exposure lower bounds, but for incremental tilts and edge-dose limits rather than the interior fixed-dose partial mean. BibautVanderLaan2017AdaptiveSmoothing explains why closing the rate frontier matters for inference: fixed-dose dose-response values are nonregular, so any later adaptive confidence procedure must know the sharp benchmark it is adapting to. CinelliEtAl2025CausalChallenges underscores that minimax optimality for modern causal functionals remains a live open problem rather than a settled technicality.
honest_scope: The settled kernel in this version is the pointwise lower bound in the common-covariate-smoothness class with beta >= alpha. The proposal does not claim the full anisotropic phase diagram, integrated-risk sharpness, or adaptive confidence bounds. Those are downstream consumers of the converse. The explicit open frontier is whether beta enters the true rate once beta < alpha, since Bonvini and Kennedy themselves suggest the current beta dependence in the upper analysis may be partly an artifact. The special-case reduction s >= d/4 is included to satisfy the sanity check against classical interior nonparametric regression.

seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Prove a sharp minimax lower bound for the interior fixed-dose value theta(t)=E[mu(t,X)] over anisotropic Holder classes that matches the Bonvini-Kennedy higher-order influence-function upper rate.
seed_details.summary: Match the published HOIF upper rate from below at a fixed interior dose on the beta >= alpha subclass with common covariate smoothness s.
seed_details.anchor_read: Bonvini-Kennedy introduction states that a tight lower bound remains elusive and identifies minimax optimality as a natural next step; their Section 3.4 discussion isolates the beta >= alpha, common-s regime where the upper rate is explicit.
seed_details.red_team: Immediate from the CATE minimax paper because both are regression-functional hybrids.
seed_details.rebuttal: The CATE construction does not preserve continuous-treatment density normalization, local positivity in a dose neighborhood, or the fixed-dose partial-mean geometry.

seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Determine the complete anisotropic phase diagram for pointwise and integrated interior dose-response risk, including the transition boundaries where oracle regression, HOIF nuisance products, or conditional-density estimation dominates.
seed_details.summary: Characterize every elbow in alpha, beta, and s for pointwise interior risk on one law class.
seed_details.anchor_read: Bonvini-Kennedy frame the problem exactly as a competition between oracle smoothing and nuisance-product terms, but do not supply a converse or a complete elbow map.
seed_details.red_team: This is just a longer restatement of the first seed without extra math.
seed_details.rebuttal: A complete phase diagram would have to prove which lower-bound channel dominates in each regime and whether beta creates a genuinely new phase.

seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Determine the complete anisotropic phase diagram for pointwise and integrated interior dose-response risk, including the transition boundaries where oracle regression, HOIF nuisance products, or conditional-density estimation dominates.
seed_details.summary: Prove the sharp integrated L2 risk frontier and compare it to the pointwise frontier.
seed_details.anchor_read: The Bonvini-Kennedy setup distinguishes pointwise and integrated losses but leaves both converse questions open; the CATE discussion suggests L2 and pointwise rates can differ in how localization enters.
seed_details.red_team: Integrated risk is a routine integral of the pointwise result.
seed_details.rebuttal: Integrated loss changes the localization geometry and can eliminate or soften the fixed-dose kernel barrier, so a separate converse is needed.

seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Determine the complete anisotropic phase diagram for pointwise and integrated interior dose-response risk, including the transition boundaries where oracle regression, HOIF nuisance products, or conditional-density estimation dominates.
seed_details.summary: Deliver one theorem family covering both pointwise and integrated risk with explicit elbow boundaries.
seed_details.anchor_read: The CATE paper's discussion flags other loss functions as open, and the dose-response paper already separates local and global estimators without a unified frontier.
seed_details.red_team: Too broad to be a first cold-start angle.
seed_details.rebuttal: The unified statement is mathematically coherent, but it likely depends on first solving the narrower pointwise converse.

seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Determine the complete anisotropic phase diagram for pointwise and integrated interior dose-response risk, including the transition boundaries where oracle regression, HOIF nuisance products, or conditional-density estimation dominates.
seed_details.summary: Compare the minimax frontier when the marginal or joint treatment density is known versus unknown, to certify whether the density channel is structural.
seed_details.anchor_read: Bonvini-Kennedy's upper analysis isolates a remainder tied to estimating the density side of the problem, but leaves open whether that is a proof artifact or a true frontier shift.
seed_details.red_team: This only diagnoses the proof, not the parameter.
seed_details.rebuttal: If the lower frontier changes across known-density and unknown-density classes, that is a structural statement about the parameter's information geometry.

seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Prove a formal separation between minimax lower bounds for incremental continuous-exposure effects or edge-dose limits and the unresolved interior fixed-dose dose-response minimax lower bound.
seed_details.summary: Show that incremental and edge-dose lower bounds do not reduce to the interior fixed-dose problem.
seed_details.anchor_read: Schindl-Shen-Kennedy emphasize delta-scaled effective sample size and an edge-dose limit, then list higher-smoothness interior dose-response questions as future work.
seed_details.red_team: The estimands are obviously different, so formalizing the distinction adds little.
seed_details.rebuttal: A theorem-level counterexample would stop referees from citing incremental lower bounds as a surrogate solution to the interior minimax gap.

seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Establish whether adaptive honest confidence intervals or bands for the interior dose-response curve can attain the sharp minimax rate when the anisotropic smoothness vector (alpha,beta,s) is unknown.
seed_details.summary: After fixing the frontier, prove the unavoidable adaptation penalty for honest fixed-dose intervals over unknown smoothness.
seed_details.anchor_read: Bibaut and van der Laan treat fixed-dose dose-response as a nonregular smoothing problem and derive adaptive intervals, but not against the Bonvini-Kennedy anisotropic frontier.
seed_details.red_team: This is premature because the base rate frontier is still unresolved.
seed_details.rebuttal: It becomes viable on a solved subclass, but it is not the best first angle for the current qid.

seed_details.anchor_open_problem: Establish whether adaptive honest confidence intervals or bands for the interior dose-response curve can attain the sharp minimax rate when the anisotropic smoothness vector (alpha,beta,s) is unknown.
seed_details.summary: Construct Lepski-style honest intervals on a self-similar subclass adapted to the continuous-dose nuisance geometry.
seed_details.anchor_read: Bibaut and van der Laan already provide a generic adaptive-smoothing route, so the distinct open content is specific to the continuous-treatment nuisance structure.
seed_details.red_team: This looks like a direct implementation of existing adaptive smoothing ideas.
seed_details.rebuttal: The nuisance-density interaction makes the bias bookkeeping nontrivial, but the kernel is still weaker than the converse itself.

literature_map.anchor: BonviniKennedy2022FastDRF is the direct open-problem source: the introduction says a tight minimax lower bound remains elusive, Section 3.4 isolates the explicit beta >= alpha rate regime, and the later discussion keeps the sharp converse open.
adjacent_threads: KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2022MinimaxCATE gives the closest lower-bound architecture: localized fuzzy hypotheses combining a regression bump with nuisance perturbations.

RobinsLiTchetgenVanderVaart2008HOIF and RobinsLiTchetgenVanderVaart2016HOIFTechnical explain why the relevant upper frontier is a higher-order nonlinear-functional frontier rather than a first-order DML one.

ColangeloLee2020ContinuousDML and SemenovaChernozhukov2017CausalFunctions are first-order orthogonal-score comparators, useful for delimiting what is already settled on asymptotic normality but not on minimax optimality.

SchindlShenKennedy2024IncrementalContinuous proves genuine continuous-exposure lower bounds, but only for incremental tilts and edge-dose limits, confirming that no published interior fixed-dose converse has already appeared.
BibautVanderLaan2017AdaptiveSmoothing shows why the converse matters downstream: honest inference for fixed-dose dose-response values must adapt to a nonregular benchmark.

novelty_justification.precise_gap: The first paper gives the upper side but no converse; the second gives the right lower-bound technology but for a binary-treatment CATE target without continuous-dose density normalization, interior-dose localization, or the partial-mean structure theta_P(t_0) = int mu_P(t_0,x) dP_X(x).
novelty_justification.why_not_subsumed: No paper located in the scan proves a minimax lower bound for the interior fixed-dose continuous-treatment mean response. Incremental-effect lower bounds are for a different estimand and effective-sample-size geometry, and the continuous-treatment DML papers establish first-order inference rather than a matching minimax frontier.
novelty_justification.chosen_kernel_reason: The beta >= alpha pointwise converse is the highest-scoring seed that survives the scan: it is sharp, theorem-level, close enough to the published upper result to be feasible, and still leaves honest open content in the unresolved full beta-frontier.
